title: Remote message send — Pid ! Msg across nodes
purpose: The fundamental distribution operation: sending a message to a process on another node. When the target PID is remote, the message is ETF-encoded, wrapped in a SEND control message, and written to the connection. The remote node decodes the message and delivers it to the target process's mailbox.
task: Implement remote message send and the SEND/REG_SEND control message handlers.

requirements.title: Remote send dispatch
requirements.spec: When the send instruction (or erlang:send/2) targets a remote PID, route the message through the distribution layer instead of the local mailbox.
acceptance: send to remote PID encodes the message via ETF and writes to the connection
send to local PID is unchanged (existing behavior)
If no connection exists to the target node, attempt to connect (or return noconnection)
Test: send message from node A to node B, verify delivery

requirements.title: SEND and REG_SEND control messages
requirements.spec: Implement the distribution control message types for message delivery.
acceptance: SEND control: {2, Cookie, ToPid} + message payload — delivers to PID on receiving node
REG_SEND control: {6, FromPid, Cookie, ToName} + message payload — delivers to registered name
Incoming SEND decodes the message and enqueues in target PID's mailbox
Incoming REG_SEND resolves the name via process registry
Test: send via SEND control, verify mailbox delivery

requirements.title: Integration test — cross-node message passing
requirements.spec: End-to-end test with two beamr instances communicating.
acceptance: Test spawns two beamr schedulers with different node names
Node A sends a message to a PID on node B
Node B's process receives the message in its mailbox
Round-trip: B replies to A, A receives the reply

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement port-based distribution — only PID-to-PID messaging
SHALL NOT implement message ordering guarantees beyond TCP's in-order delivery
SHALL handle connection failure during send (return noconnection error)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
